perturbation.original_text: HPS shall pay such amount to SHPS within fifteen days following the end of the applicable month.
perturbation.changed_text: HPS shall pay such amount to SHPS within forty-five days following the end of the applicable month.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the payment term from 'fifteen days' to 'forty-five days' creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2.4, which also states that HPS shall pay SHPS within fifteen days following the end of each month. This creates uncertainty about the correct payment deadline, as either section could be argued as the valid one. This also contradicts the general payment terms established in Section 2.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HPS shall pay such amount to SHPS within fifteen days following the end of each month. At the time of payment HPS shall submit to SHPS a schedule for the month of payment setting forth the calculation of fees payable under this Section 2.4 and Care 2 3 Management Revenues by New Client. For convenience, the parties acknowledge that HPS will deduct amounts owed by SHPS under this Section 2.4 from the amounts owed by HPS under this Section 2.4 and reflect such deductions in the schedule prepared by HPS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment term from 'fifteen days' to 'forty-five days'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'HPS shall pay such amount to SHPS within fifteen days following the end of each month.' This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the payment deadline, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable as either section could be argued as valid.

perturbation.original_text: Unless either party gives the other at least ninety days' prior written notice that it has elected not to extend the term of this Agreement beyond December 31, 1998, the term of this Agreement will be automatically extended until December 31, 1999.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless either party gives the other at least thirty days' prior written notice that it has elected not to extend the term of this Agreement beyond December 31, 1998, the term of this Agreement will be automatically extended until December 31, 1999.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the notice period from 'ninety days' to 'thirty days' creates an in-text contradiction with the later part of the same section. The clause states 'Thereafter this Agreement will automatically be renewed for successive additional periods of one year, unless either party gives notice of cancellation on or before October 1 of any such year'. Therefore, if it says 30 days for 1998, and October 1 of any year, for renewal period. This contradiction will lead to uncertainty on the termination notice period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERM. The term of this Agreement will commence on January 1, 1998 (the "Effective Date") and will end on December 31, 1998. Unless either party gives the other at least thirty days' prior written notice that it has elected not to extend the term of this Agreement beyond December 31, 1998, the term of this Agreement will be automatically extended until December 31, 1999. Thereafter this Agreement will automatically be renewed for successive additional periods of one year, unless either party gives notice of cancellation on or before October 1 of any such year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period from 'ninety days' to 'thirty days' for the initial term's extension. The contradicted text, which contains the changed text, states that for subsequent renewals, notice of cancellation must be given 'on or before October 1 of any such year'. This creates an inconsistency because the 30-day notice applies to the first extension but the October 1st deadline applies to subsequent renewals. This leads to a contradiction regarding the timing of the notice period, resulting in confusion about which deadline applies in which scenario.

perturbation.original_text: HPS agrees to outsource to SHPS, and hereby appoints SHPS as the exclusive provider of, Care Management Services to the Clients, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: HPS agrees to outsource to SHPS, and hereby appoints SHPS as a provider of, Care Management Services to the Clients, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'exclusive', SHPS is no longer the sole provider of Care Management Services. This modification introduces an in-text contradiction with the core concept of the agreement that SHPS will be the single company providing services. This would create uncertainty on SHPS's duties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AGREEMENT TO OUTSOURCE CARE MANAGEMENT SERVICES. HPS agrees to outsource to SHPS, and hereby appoints SHPS as a provider of, Care Management Services to the Clients, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement. SHPS shall provide the Care Management Services directly to the Clients in accordance with the terms of the Client Agreements. "Care Management Services" means the business of providing utilization review (which includes, but is not limited to, pre-admission certification, prior authorization, prospective length of stay approvals, second opinions, concurrent review and discharge planning), catastrophic medical case management, disease management and demand management (24 hours per day, 7 days per week) services to benefits payors and health providers, in all cases in accordance with the terms of the applicable Client Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text appointed SHPS as the *exclusive* provider, implying a sole-source relationship. The changed text removes 'exclusive,' making SHPS *a* provider, not necessarily the only one. The contradicted text begins with 'AGREEMENT TO OUTSOURCE CARE MANAGEMENT SERVICES' where HPS is outsourcing to SHPS, and hereby appoints SHPS as *a* provider. If SHPS is not the exclusive provider, it introduces the possibility of other providers, directly contradicting the intent of outsourcing Care Management Services to *SHPS* specifically. The 'Care Management Services' definition in the contradicted text also refers to services that *SHPS* shall provide which becomes problematic if other providers are also offering such services. This causes ambiguity and creates a contradiction. The 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition supports that different sections of a contract providing conflicting obligations creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced.
